distributed machine learning.question: What is distributed machine learning?
Why is distributed training used?
What is data parallelism?
What is model parallelism?
What is the difference between data parallelism and model parallelism?
Why are GPUs commonly used for machine learning training?
What is a TPU, and how does it differ from a GPU?
What is distributed inference?
Why is communication between machines important during distributed training?
What challenges can arise during distributed machine learning?
Explain how data parallelism works during neural network training.
When would you choose model parallelism instead of data parallelism?
What is a parameter server architecture, and when is it useful?
Compare synchronous and asynchronous distributed training.
How does communication overhead affect distributed training performance?
How would you determine whether distributed training is worth using for a particular project?
Compare PyTorch Distributed, TensorFlow Distributed, and Horovod.
How would you monitor the performance of a distributed training job?
How would you reduce communication bottlenecks during distributed training?
Explain the trade-offs between increasing the number of GPUs and improving GPU utilization.
Design a distributed machine learning architecture capable of training billion-parameter models.
How would you optimize distributed training to maximize GPU utilization while minimizing communication overhead?
Explain the trade-offs between distributed training cost, scalability, and convergence speed.
How would you determine whether slow training is caused by computation, networking, or storage bottlenecks?
How would you design a fault-tolerant distributed training system?
How would you efficiently train machine learning models across multiple geographic regions?
Describe strategies for reducing cloud costs during large-scale distributed machine learning.
How would you evaluate scaling efficiency when increasing the number of GPUs or compute nodes?
How would you build a distributed inference platform capable of serving millions of requests per day?
Design the infrastructure required to support continuous distributed training and deployment for multiple machine learning teams.
A distributed training job that normally finishes in four hours suddenly takes twelve hours. Walk me through your investigation.
Your training cluster scales from four GPUs to sixteen GPUs, but training speed improves very little. Explain how you would identify the bottleneck.
One GPU repeatedly fails during distributed training. Describe how you would maintain training progress without restarting the entire job.
A stakeholder wants to reduce cloud costs while continuing to train increasingly larger machine learning models. Explain the engineering trade-offs you would evaluate.
Your distributed inference service experiences sudden traffic spikes after a product launch. Describe how you would scale the infrastructure.
Your model no longer fits into the memory of a single GPU. Walk me through the approaches you would consider.
A production inference cluster begins returning inconsistent predictions across different servers. Explain how you would investigate the issue.
Your distributed training system must support multiple teams training models simultaneously. Describe how you would manage scheduling, resource allocation, and fairness.
A global machine learning platform must continue operating even if an entire cloud region becomes unavailable. Explain how you would design for resilience.

You're responsible for designing a worldwide distributed machine learning platform capable of training and serving hundreds of models. Explain your architecture for compute, networking, storage, monitoring, fault tolerance, and scaling.
